concepts.title: Roots of a complex number
concepts.conceptBody:
Roots of a Complex Number 

Let $z=a+ib$ be written in polar form like $z=r\left( \text{cosn}\theta +i\text{sin}\theta \right)=r{{e}^{i\theta }}$

Then the nth roots of $\text{z}$ are given by

${{z}^{1/n}}={{r}^{1/n}}\left[ \text{cos}\frac{\left( 2k\pi +\theta \right)}{n}+i\text{sin}\frac{\left( 2k\pi +\theta \right)}{n} \right]$

where $\text{k}$ can take value from $0,1,2,\ldots \left( \text{n}-1 \right)$. (i.e., one less than the denominator) for $\text{k}\ge \text{n}$, the roots will repeat.

put $\text{k}=0$ in the above equation.

$\Rightarrow \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }{{\text{z}}^{1/\text{n}}}={{\text{r}}^{1/\text{n}}}\left[ \text{cos}\left( \frac{\theta }{\text{n}} \right)+\text{isin}\left( \frac{\theta }{\text{n}} \right) \right]$ …(i)

now put $\text{k}=\text{n}$

$\Rightarrow \text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }{{\text{z}}^{1/\text{n}}}={{\text{r}}^{1/\text{n}}}\left[ \text{cos}\left( \frac{2\text{n}\pi +\theta }{\text{n}} \right)+\text{isin}\left( \frac{2\text{n}\pi +\theta }{\text{n}} \right) \right]$ $={{r}^{1/n}}\left[ \text{cos}\left( \frac{2n\pi }{n}+\frac{\theta }{n} \right)+i\text{sin}\left( \frac{2n\pi }{n}+\theta \right) \right]$

$={{r}^{1/n}}\left[ \text{cos}\left( \frac{\theta }{n} \right)+i\text{sin}\left( \theta /n \right) \right]$

which is same as (i)
